title: Fuel system having serially arranged in-tank pumps
abstract: A pump system (12) is disclosed for use with a fuel system (10) of an engine (12). The pump system may have a first pump (40) with a first end (52), a second end (54), a reservoir (82) located at the second end, and at least one pump mechanism (76) configured to receive fluid from the reservoir. The pump system may also have a second pump (42) mounted to the first pump at the second end and having at least one pump mechanism (90) configured to discharge fluid into the reservoir of the first pump. The pump system may further have a mechanical input (48) operatively connected to the at least one pump mechanism of each of the first and second pumps.
claims: Claims Claims 1. A pump system (16), comprising: a first pump (40) having a first end (52), a second end (54), a reservoir (82) located at the second end, and at least one pump mechanism (76) configured to receive fluid from the reservoir; a second pump (42) mounted to the first pump at the second end and having at least one pump mechanism (90) configured to discharge fluid into the reservoir of the first pump; and a mechanical input (48) operatively connected to the at least one pump mechanism of each of the first and second pumps, 2. The pump system of claim 1, wherein the mechanical input is a shaft (62, 89) that extends through the first pump to the second pump. 3. The pump system of claim 1, further including an electric motor (44) operatively connected to the mechanical input. 4. The pump system of claim 1, wherein the first and second pumps are different types of pumps. 5. The pump system of claim 4, wherein: the first pump is a piston pump; and the second pump is a centrifugal pump having multiple impellers (90). 6. The pump system of claim 5, wherein the first and second pumps are mounted inside a fluid tank (14). 7. The pump system of claim 6, wherein an end (94) of the second pump opposite the first pump is open to the fluid tank. 8. The pump system of claim 7, wherein the second pump is located gravitationally lower in the fluid tank than the first pump. 9. The pump system of claim 8, further including a high- pressure outlet (78) extending from the second end of the first pump out of the fluid tank, wherein: the first pump is mounted to the fluid tank at only at the first end; and the second pump is mounted to only the first pump. 10. The pump system of claim 1, further including a filter (50) disposed at an inlet of the second pump.
